assertions.description: The PointerEvent constructor exists, allowing untrusted PointerEvents to be constructed in JS.
PointerEventInit accepts a coalescedEvents sequence and surfaces it via getCoalescedEvents().
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const c=new PointerEvent('pointermove',{clientX:11,clientY:22});const e=new PointerEvent('pointermove',{coalescedEvents:[c]});const list=e.getCoalescedEvents();return list.length===1 && list[0].clientX===11 && list[0].clientY===22;}catch(err){return false;}})()

assertions.description: For an untrusted PointerEvent, dispatching does not overwrite the target of its coalesced entries.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const a=document.createElement('div');const b=document.createElement('div');document.body.append(a,b);const inner=new PointerEvent('pointermove',{clientX:1,clientY:2});Object.defineProperty(inner,'target',{value:a,configurable:true});const e=new PointerEvent('pointermove',{coalescedEvents:[inner]});b.dispatchEvent(e);const got=e.getCoalescedEvents()[0];const ok=got!==e && (got.target===a||got.target===null);a.remove();b.remove();return ok;}catch(err){return false;}})()

assertions.description: PointerEventInit accepts a predictedEvents sequence; getPredictedEvents() returns the supplied list.
